jokes.text: Dennis lost a bet, and I got to redesign our old apartment. He is gonna flip!
jokes.setup: Mac won a bet and redesigned their apartment
jokes.punchline: Dennis will react strongly when he sees it
jokes.explanation: Sets up the apartment reveal later, good character work showing Mac's excitement

jokes.text: we finally get a chance to fire... ...the rocket launcher
jokes.setup: Casual mention of celebrating
jokes.punchline: With a rocket launcher that arrived in the mail
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd - ordering a rocket launcher by mail and treating it casually is peak Charlie

jokes.text: Are we us? Well, yeah, of course you are. I don't want to be me. Can I be someone else? No. You have to be you
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining his fake identity scheme
jokes.punchline: Charlie's existential confusion about identity while Mac wants to escape being himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - Charlie's philosophical confusion and Mac's self-loathing are perfectly in character

jokes.text: Are you right-handed, or left-handed? That won't come up. Well, it could come up. It won't! Okay. I'm gonna go with left then.
jokes.setup: Mac worrying about getting his fake identity details right
jokes.punchline: Obsessing over irrelevant handedness details Dennis insists won't matter
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the immediate payoff when handedness is mentioned to Mandy

jokes.text: So, this is the hangout spot then, huh? Yeah, yeah, yeah, these are my friends. Oh, so that must make you Prudence then. Yes. Uh-huh. Yes, it does. Yeah. Oh. He is Prudence, and I'm Griffin. No, he's Mac!
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to maintain his Brian identity with fake names for everyone
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately blowing the fake names by correcting himself
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe comedy - the scheme immediately starts falling apart due to Mac's inability to maintain the lie

jokes.text: And he's left-handed. Yeah. Oh. I suppose I didn't know that about you. Yeah. No, well, there was really no reason for it to come up.
jokes.setup: Mac's earlier obsession with establishing handedness
jokes.punchline: It immediately comes up and creates awkward questions
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - the seemingly random detail immediately becomes relevant and awkward

jokes.text: And this is, uh... this is little Brian Junior. Oh. Yeah. He's my son.
jokes.setup: Dennis introducing his fake identity to friends
jokes.punchline: Revealing he has a child with this woman, shocking everyone
jokes.explanation: Major escalation - drops the bombshell casually while everyone processes the implications

jokes.text: I picked up Mandy there at an Applebee's. Oh. I was pretending to be an airline pilot who flies out of Philly named Brian LeFevre.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining how he met Mandy
jokes.punchline: The elaborate lie involving a fake pilot identity at Applebee's
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis behavior - elaborate manipulation scheme for a one-night stand at Applebee's

jokes.text: Yes, but in this scenario, she's my sister. See, I'm always tweaking things based on the situation.
jokes.setup: Question about his previous fake backstory
jokes.punchline: Casually changing major family relationships in his lies
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work - Dennis treating massive lies as minor adjustments shows his sociopathy

jokes.text: Look, as we all know, everything is about emotion with women. Right, Dee? What?! Whoa. Whoa. Oh, my God. Whoa!
jokes.setup: Mac making a sexist generalization
jokes.punchline: Dee's explosive reaction to being used as validation for sexism
jokes.explanation: Good reaction comedy - Dee's outrage at being used to validate Mac's casual sexism

jokes.text: maybe we got to close the emotion door for Mandy by telling her that you're gay. Yeah, right, but I already had sex with her. How am I gonna convince her I'm gay? Just go with me here. Brian's not above having a one-night stand with a woman, but he's got no room for her emotionally because he's in love with me. With you?
jokes.setup: Mac suggesting Dennis pretend to be gay
jokes.punchline: Mac inserting himself as Dennis's gay lover in the fake scenario
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac behavior - his repressed sexuality coming through in his 'helpful' suggestions

jokes.text: That plan stinks, because it's always about money with women. That's all there is to it. So I think the best thing to do is to find a guy who's got more money than you, like in that movie, uh, Indecent Proposal.
jokes.setup: Frank dismissing Mac's emotion-based plan
jokes.punchline: Suggesting recreating Indecent Proposal as a real solution
jokes.explanation: Good Frank moment - treating a movie plot as a practical business solution

jokes.text: Would you sleep with her for a million dollars? No way. It's too much money.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking if Frank would sleep with Mandy for a million
jokes.punchline: Frank refusing because the amount is too HIGH, not too low
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - subverts expectation by Frank thinking the amount is excessive rather than insufficient

jokes.text: I've got a great plan, but, uh, I'm thinking that this isn't my problem, and, also, you guys wouldn't listen to me no matter what I said. Good point.
jokes.setup: Dee offering to help with a plan
jokes.punchline: Accurately predicting they'll dismiss her, which they immediately do
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor - Dee acknowledging the group dynamic while it plays out in real time

jokes.text: I got it. If I just get the waitress pregnant, she's gonna be tied to me for life, so, I got to do that. He's already on to another thing.
jokes.setup: Charlie being asked for a plan to help Dennis
jokes.punchline: Charlie completely ignoring the problem to focus on his own waitress obsession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie - completely self-absorbed and unable to focus on anyone else's problems

jokes.text: Surprise! Huh. It looks, uh... Exactly the same. I was able to track down versions of our old furniture, and even found replicas of all the knickknacks that burned in the fire. It's bizarre. Bizarre in a good way. No.
jokes.setup: Mac excitedly revealing his apartment redesign
jokes.punchline: He recreated everything exactly as it was, thinking this was an improvement
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag concept - Mac's idea of redesign is perfect restoration, missing the point entirely

jokes.text: there's really no easy way to say this, but, um, Mac and I aren't just friends. He's actually my, uh... Lover. Partner. Yes, well, we do make love. We don't actually make love... He's the power bottom.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to fake being gay with Mac
jokes.punchline: Mac enthusiastically adding sexual details Dennis doesn't want to include
jokes.explanation: Great cringe comedy - Mac getting too invested in the fake relationship while Dennis tries to backpedal

jokes.text: Now he generates a tremendous amount of power... ...from the bottom. Don't sell yourself short. He does. No, no. It's not that I... It's just that we don't have sex.
jokes.setup: Mac describing Dennis as a power bottom
jokes.punchline: Mac insisting on the details while Dennis desperately tries to clarify they don't actually have sex
jokes.explanation: Escalating the cringe - Mac's repressed sexuality coming through as he gets more detailed about their fake relationship

jokes.text: the only thing I can think of better than Junior having one dad is, well, him having two. Ee. Yeah. No. Wow. I wasn't expecting that.
jokes.setup: Mandy processing that Dennis is gay and with Mac
jokes.punchline: She suggests all three of them raise the child together, horrifying Dennis
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - the plan backfires spectacularly when Mandy embraces the gay couple angle

jokes.text: All right, Charlie, you got 15 minutes, and it's a dollar a minute starting... now. Oh, no. No, no, no, no. No, we're not doing this. Uh-uh.
jokes.setup: Charlie showing up at the waitress's door
jokes.punchline: She treats their interaction like prostitution with a meter running
jokes.explanation: Good character work - shows how transactional the waitress has to be with Charlie due to his stalking

jokes.text: It's not about sex. Okay, good. It's about having a baby. And I'm gone.
jokes.setup: Charlie clarifying it's not about sex
jokes.punchline: Making it worse by saying it's about getting her pregnant
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure - Charlie thinking he's making it better while making it infinitely worse

jokes.text: All the way to sitting on the beach in Rio de Janeiro. Driving the lane to fame.
jokes.setup: Charlie describing the good life Frank's money could provide
jokes.punchline: His nonsensical mixed metaphors and confused imagery
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie word salad mixing vacation imagery with sports metaphors

jokes.text: Look, I know you've always wanted a baby, right? I see you. You poke around the preschools and the playgrounds, and you got that longing look in your eye. Oh, God, that's so creepy.
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to convince the waitress she wants a baby
jokes.punchline: Describing her behavior in the creepiest possible way while thinking it's romantic
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - Charlie's romantic delusions making normal behavior sound predatory

jokes.text: It's not gonna happen for you. Excuse me? Yeah, you ain't gonna have a baby. Check this out. Your life has been on a slow, but steady decline for years.
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to convince her she wants a baby
jokes.punchline: Brutally listing why her life is getting worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Charlie's 'romantic pitch' becoming increasingly insulting

jokes.text: Well... 'cause there is no one better. And... I love you.
jokes.setup: Waitress asking why Charlie is obsessed with her when she's a mess
jokes.punchline: Charlie's genuine, delusional love confession after insulting her
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Charlie's twisted romanticism coming through after he's been terrible

jokes.text: What are you doing, Frank? Oh, Charlie's doing a thing at the apartment tonight, so I'm gonna crash here. Okay? No, get a hotel.
jokes.setup: Frank appearing at Dee's apartment
jokes.punchline: Casually inviting himself to stay without asking
jokes.explanation: Standard Frank behavior - entitled and boundary-crossing

jokes.text: You know what, do you mind if I take the bed? Because I got a little pinched nerve in my neck. It's driving me crazy. Wh... yeah, I mind.
jokes.setup: Frank asking to crash at Dee's
jokes.punchline: Immediately trying to take her bed with a flimsy excuse
jokes.explanation: Good Frank escalation - not content with just staying, he wants her bed too

jokes.text: Don't get all emotional, just do it. I don't want this.
jokes.setup: Frank demanding Dee make him popcorn
jokes.punchline: Dismissing her reasonable objections as being 'emotional'
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank manipulation - gaslighting Dee's reasonable boundaries

jokes.text: Maybe we should do it. W-we? We are not a couple, Mac. Well, no, we would just keep pretending.
jokes.setup: Mac suggesting they raise the child
jokes.punchline: Mac wanting to continue the fake relationship indefinitely
jokes.explanation: Great character work - Mac's repressed feelings coming through as he gets attached to the fake relationship

jokes.text: I am not gonna pretend to be in a relationship with you for the entirety of my child's life. Are you insane?
jokes.setup: Mac wanting to continue the fake relationship
jokes.punchline: Dennis's horrified realization of what Mac is suggesting
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat - Dennis processing the long-term implications of Mac's suggestion

jokes.text: What is this? This is the gym. Uh-huh. And where were you going to sleep? Well, I figured since we got so used to sleeping in the same bed at Dee's, that we could just sort of continue that here.
jokes.setup: Dennis finding Mac's sleeping arrangement
jokes.punchline: Mac has set up a 'gym' and wants to continue sharing a bed
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Mac's attachment - he's set up a home gym expecting to live together

jokes.text: You want to sleep on the couch... ...together. I'm gonna sleep next to the dildo bike. The Ass-pounder, uh, 4000, is an exercise... bike.
jokes.setup: Dennis rejecting Mac's bed-sharing plan
jokes.punchline: Revealing Mac has an exercise bike called the 'Ass-pounder 4000'
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - the ridiculous name for what Mac claims is just exercise equipment

jokes.text: That was, um... That was not as bad as I expected. It was amazing. It kind of was. It kind of was amazing.
jokes.setup: Post-sex conversation between Charlie and the waitress
jokes.punchline: Both awkwardly admitting it was better than expected
jokes.explanation: Awkward post-coital moment that's both sweet and uncomfortable

jokes.text: What is this? What are you doing there? This? Oh, this just improves the chances of conception. Of what? Of getting pregnant.
jokes.setup: Charlie doing something strange after sex
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's doing something to improve pregnancy chances
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Charlie's obsession with getting her pregnant ruining the moment

jokes.text: Charlie, this... this paint could be full of lead. Are you serious? I can't carry a baby around lead paint.
jokes.setup: Waitress explaining why Charlie needs to repaint
jokes.punchline: Already acting like she's pregnant and making demands
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - the waitress immediately becoming demanding about the pregnancy

jokes.text: Look, I'm just saying if you want to be a mom, you're gonna have to learn how to control your emotions.
jokes.setup: Charlie responding to the waitress being assertive
jokes.punchline: Mansplaining motherhood to her while being completely wrong
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Charlie's immediate shift to controlling behavior after getting what he wanted

jokes.text: It's like one thing to trap a person with a baby, but then when you actually have a baby, then you become trapped.
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining his baby plan backfiring
jokes.punchline: Realizing he's trapped himself with his own trap
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - Charlie's logical progression about his own manipulative scheme

jokes.text: Don't be a whore tonight, Dee, and try and bang me again, okay? Because I got nothing left. I'm spent; I'm really exhausted. Too much sex and too much... stuff. Just don't be a whore, okay?
jokes.setup: Charlie asking to sleep in Dee's bed
jokes.punchline: Accusing Dee of trying to seduce him while he's vulnerable
jokes.explanation: Great Charlie delusion - his complete misreading of his relationship with Dee

jokes.text: Dee! Egg! Where is it? Where is it? There's no eggs here. Where are your eggs? Dee, where are your eggs? Dee, eggs! We're looking for your eggs. Where are your eggs? We can't find them!
jokes.setup: The gang looking for eggs at Dee's apartment
jokes.punchline: Increasingly frantic demands for eggs while treating Dee like she's hiding them
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the egg obsession into group hysteria

jokes.text: You have been stalking that woman for 15 years. Now she's finally showing you some attention, and you don't like it?
jokes.setup: Charlie complaining about the waitress being clingy
jokes.punchline: Dee pointing out the obvious irony of his complaint
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony - Dee cutting through Charlie's hypocrisy with perfect economy

jokes.text: Do you want to take care of this child for the rest of your life, is that what you want? Or do you want me to get out of this situation that I'm in? I'm gonna smash it!
jokes.setup: Dennis pressuring Dee to help with the baby
jokes.punchline: Threatening the child while demanding help with childcare
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Dennis's threats becoming increasingly unhinged

jokes.text: I'm gonna offer you $5,000. What are you doing, Frank? Look, I'm gonna give you $5,000, we bang, you take the money, you go up, you get on a plane, you fly to wherever the hell it is in the sticks, and, uh, you know, $5,000 goes a long way up there.
jokes.setup: Frank presenting his 'millionaire' offer
jokes.punchline: Drastically lowballing the Indecent Proposal concept with crude delivery
jokes.explanation: Good Frank character work - completely missing the point of the movie reference

jokes.text: If you need more money, call me up, come back, we bang again. Price goes down 'cause I already had you. But if you want to bring a girlfriend... price goes back up.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining his payment structure
jokes.punchline: Treating it like a business with repeat customer discounts and upselling
jokes.explanation: Excellent Frank escalation - applying business logic to prostitution with casual efficiency

jokes.text: Five grand is a lot of money... is that offer just out on the table? I'm not gonna bang you. I don't want to bang you, either, Frank, but I'll power through for five grand.
jokes.setup: Mac hearing Frank's offer to Mandy
jokes.punchline: Mac volunteering to take Frank's offer despite both being uninterested
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment - Mac's practical approach to unwanted prostitution for money

jokes.text: Dennis is his sex slave name. He's my gimp. What? No. I... I don't have a sex slave name. I'm not...
jokes.setup: Frank trying to cover Dennis's name slip
jokes.punchline: Making it worse by inventing sexual role-play details
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Frank making the cover story worse with unnecessary sexual details

jokes.text: My name... my name isn't Brian LeFevre. My real name... is Dane Brass. And I hold top secret government information. Okay? They're after me
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to get rid of Mandy permanently
jokes.punchline: Inventing an elaborate spy backstory to explain everything
jokes.explanation: Good Dennis escalation - when manipulation fails, he goes full dramatic fiction

jokes.text: Oh, oh. I've been shot. Well, I didn't hear any gun... Oh, well... yeah, not there it is there. That was the sound of it. Normally you'd... you'd hear it before you see the blood, but, uh, not in this case.
jokes.setup: Dennis faking being shot
jokes.punchline: Poorly improvising gunshot sound effects and physics when called out
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Dennis's elaborate lie falling apart in real time

jokes.text: Not a regular hospital, though, 'cause technically, he's not supposed to exist. A bad guy one. Oh, no, I've lost my lover. I'll need a new gimp!
jokes.setup: The gang playing along with Dennis's spy story
jokes.punchline: Mac and Frank enthusiastically adding to the fiction with their own absurd details
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - the gang getting carried away with their own improv

jokes.text: You big city folks sure like to complicate things, don't ya?
jokes.setup: The elaborate spy fiction and fake death
jokes.punchline: Mandy calmly seeing through all of it as unnecessary drama
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - Mandy's simple wisdom cutting through all their elaborate nonsense

jokes.text: Bye, Dada.
jokes.setup: Dennis saying goodbye to his son
jokes.punchline: The child calling him 'Dada' as he tries to escape responsibility
jokes.explanation: Powerful emotional moment - the innocent child's goodbye cutting through Dennis's manipulation

jokes.text: I can't do this. What are you talking about, bud? The plan worked. No, this... no, this-this is crazy. What are we doing? I can't... I-I can't... I can't do any of this shit anymore.
jokes.setup: Dennis having successfully gotten rid of Mandy
jokes.punchline: Having a breakdown about his life choices instead of celebrating
jokes.explanation: Great character development - Dennis having an actual moment of self-awareness

jokes.text: Because I don't want my kid to grow up like I did. With some asshole dad who was never even around. You talking about me or Bruce? You!
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining why he wants to be a good father
jokes.punchline: Frank asking which of Dennis's father figures he's insulting
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - Frank's casual acknowledgment of being a terrible father

jokes.text: The bar's done. Well, hang on a second. Actually, it's not done. It's not done, no. We're still in here.
jokes.setup: Dennis dramatically declaring the bar finished
jokes.punchline: The gang pointing out they're literally still there
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Dennis's dramatic moment with practical reality

jokes.text: Shh, just let me... Don't start talking, I can tell... We weren't gonna say anything. The pause is too big.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to have a dramatic exit moment
jokes.punchline: Being unable to maintain the dramatic pause because he knows them too well
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta humor - Dennis's self-awareness about dramatic timing and group dynamics

jokes.text: What a shot, man! Oh, my God! Whoa!
jokes.setup: The gang finally firing the rocket launcher
jokes.punchline: Their excited reaction to actually using it
jokes.explanation: Simple callback payoff - they finally get to use the rocket launcher they mentioned at the start
